summary: 10-phase implementation plan for layered representation theorem architecture: base parametric layer (existing), dense layer (TM+DN, D=Q), discrete layer (TM+DF, D=Z as extension). Primary deliverable is dense completeness via fragment-first architecture bypassing Int-chain obstacle.

artifacts.summary: 10-phase plan covering: restricted validity definitions, DN/DF axioms, DP derivation, soundness proofs, DenselyOrdered on quotient, Cantor's theorem application, dense completeness theorem

key_deliverables: DP derived from DF via temporal_duality
DenselyOrdered proof on BidirectionalQuotient from DN
Quotient isomorphic to Q via Cantor's theorem
deferred_work: Discrete path (TM+DF) completeness requires Archimedeanness proof
